edibility: Edible only when interior is pure white; sliced and fried; do NOT eat once interior is yellow/green/brown. Young specimens must be completely firm throughout to ensure safety.
taste: Mild, slightly nutty flavor with a somewhat marshmallow-like texture when fresh. Best enjoyed when cooked rather than raw.
search: Found in late summer through fall in woodlands, meadows, and grasslands across temperate regions. Identify by distinctive pear shape, netted surface pattern, and white interior when young. Dangerous look-alikes include young Amanita muscaria in its 'egg' stage and the toxic pigskin puffball (Scleroderma species), which has thick, dark interior.

toxicity.symptoms: Mature spore mass can cause lycoperdonosis (inhalation-related lung inflammation) if large quantities are inhaled; culinary use does not present inhalation risk
toxicity.onset: Hours to days after significant inhalation exposure
toxicity.treatmentWindow: Seek medical attention if respiratory symptoms develop after inhalation of spore cloud
toxicity.antidote: Supportive respiratory care and corticosteroids for confirmed lycoperdonosis; hospital evaluation recommended
